qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Workflow import-by-URL performs unrestricted server-side fetches
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is about the Merge node's SQL query mode enabling arbitrary code execution and arbitrary file write on the n8n server. This finding is a separate authenticated SSRF in `GET /workflows/from-url` and does not identify the Merge node, SQL query mode, or any equivalent code-execution path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory-described Merge-node SQL-query remote code execution and arbitrary file-write root cause.
evidence: The advisory summary states that authenticated users who can create or modify workflows can abuse the Merge node's SQL query mode to execute arbitrary code and write arbitrary files on the n8n server.
The session manifest lists only one final `qualifying_vuln` finding: `workflow_import_url_ssrf`.
That finding reports an authenticated SSRF in `GET /workflows/from-url` and its evidence points to the workflow import-by-URL controller, not the Merge node or any SQL query execution path.
